title: salix mucronata subsp. woodii
intro: Salix mucronata subsp. woodii, also known as the Wood's willow, is a subspecies of the white willow tree, native to southern Africa. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat pain and inflammation due to its high salicin content. The health benefits of Salix mucronata subsp. woodii include pain relief, fever reduction, and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a traditional remedy for various ailments. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application or oral consumption.
